title: Digest header comparison is variable-time string equality
description: server.rs:782: 'if actual_b64 \!= expected_b64' uses Rust's default string PartialEq which short-circuits on the first differing byte. The Digest header value is SHA-256(body) encoded as base64 and is covered by the HTTP signature, so it acts as a MAC tag over the request body. A timing channel here lets an attacker iteratively probe whether a modified body produces a Digest prefix that matches, byte-by-byte in base64. This is low-severity in practice because the signature must also verify, but the combination of signature replay window (5 min) plus this channel is a concrete attack path. Should use subtle::ConstantTimeEq on the raw digest bytes.
close_reason: Fixed: digest comparison now uses subtle::ConstantTimeEq on raw bytes.
